lemma diagonalHasLaw (n : ℕ) (i : Fin n) :
    HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
  have h₁ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₂ : ∀ (ω : WignerSpace n), (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i = ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n) := by
      intro ω
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try omega)
      <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;>
        aesop
      })
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the fact that the diagonal entries follow the distribution ν
      have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        -- Use the product measure properties to extract the distribution of a single coordinate
        have h₅ : Measurable (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) := by
          apply measurable_fst
        -- Use the fact that the product measure assigns ν to the diagonal entries
        have h₆ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
          apply Measure.pi_hasLaw
        -- Simplify the measure to ν
        have h₇ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n) = ν := by
          dsimp [wignerMeasureDist]
          simp [eq_self_iff_true, if_true]
        rw [h₇] at h₆
        exact h₆
      exact h₄
    -- Use the fact that the function (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) is equal to (fun ω ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) to transfer the law
    have h₈ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) := by
      funext ω
      rw [h₂ ω]
    rw [h₈]
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_mk]
    <;> aesop
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      apply Measure.pi_hasLaw
      <;> simp
    have h₄ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₄] at h₃
    exact h₃
  exact h₂

funext ω
    dsimp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', Matrix.of_apply, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> simp [if_pos, eq_self_iff_true, and_true]
    <;> rfl
  
  have h2 : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [Fin.ext_iff]⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [show (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = (Measure.pi fun (p : Index n) => if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ν else μ) by rfl]
    -- Use the fact that the coordinate function has the same distribution as the corresponding measure in the product
    have h : HasLaw (fun (ω : Index n → ℝ) => ω ⟨(i, i), by
        simp [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;>
        omega⟩) (if (⟨(i, i), by simp [Fin.ext_iff]⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, i), by simp [Fin.ext_iff]⟩ : Index n).val.2 then ν else μ) (Measure.pi fun (p : Index n) => if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ν else μ) := by
      -- Use the basic property of product measures
      apply hasLaw_pi
      <;> simp_all [Index]
      <;> aesop
    -- Simplify the condition for the diagonal element
    have h₁ : (⟨(i, i), by simp [Fin.ext_iff]⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, i), by simp [Fin.ext_iff]⟩ : Index n).val.2 := by
      simp [Index, Fin.ext_iff]
      <;>
      aesop
    -- Conclude the proof by simplifying the if-then-else condition
    convert h using 1 <;> simp_all
  
  have h3 : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [h1]
    exact h2
  
  exact h3

ext1 ω
    -- Simplify the expression using the definition of wignerMatrixEntryFunction and wignerMatrixMap
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction, Matrix.of_apply]
    <;> aesop
  rw [h₁]
  -- Use the fact that the projection function has law ν
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- Use the definition of WignerMeasure and pi_measurability to get the law of the projection
    have h₃ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) := rfl
    rw [h₃]
    -- Use the fact that the projection function has law (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)
    have h₄ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> simp_all [Index.mk.injEq]
      <;> aesop
    -- Use the HasLaw.prod_mk to get the law of the projection
    have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
      apply hasLaw_mk
    rw [h₄] at h₅
    exact h₅
  exact h₂

funext ω
    rw [wignerMatrixMap]
    rw [wignerMatrixMap']
    simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    (try cases n <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try {
      aesop
    }) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;>
      omega
    })
    <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;>
      omega
    })
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₄ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) = Proj.fst (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n) : WignerSpace n → ℝ := by
        ext ω
        rfl
      rw [h₄]
      apply MeasurableSpace.mapMeasurableMeasurability
      <;>
      (try simp [wignerMeasureDist]) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try {
        apply MeasureTheory.projMeasurable
      })
      <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [WignerMeasure]
      })
      <;>
      (try {
        aesop
      })
    have h₅ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;>
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;>
      (try { aesop })
      <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;>
        aesop
      })
    rw [h₅] at h₃
    exact h₃
  exact h₂

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
    <;>
    norm_num
    <;>
    aesop
  
  have h_main : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [h]
    have h₁ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the fact that the measure on the product space is the product of the measures on each component
      -- and that the diagonal entries are assigned the measure ν.
      have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        -- Use the property of the product measure to get the law of each component
        apply hasLaw_fst
      -- Simplify the measure assigned to the diagonal entry
      have h₃ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩ = ν := by
        simp [wignerMeasureDist]
        <;>
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;>
        norm_num
        <;>
        aesop
      -- Conclude that the law is ν
      rw [h₃] at h₂
      exact h₂
    exact h₁
  
  exact h_main

-- Use the fact that the diagonal entry is directly mapped to ν
    apply hasLaw_of_measurable_of_forall_ae
    · -- Prove that the function is measurable
      apply measurable_eval
    · -- Prove that the measure of the function is ν
      intro a
      simp [wignerMeasureDist, WignerMeasure, Measure.pi, Measure.normedSpace]
      <;>
      simp_all [prod_eq]
      <;>
      aesop
  -- Show that the diagonal entry of the Wigner matrix is the same as the diagonal entry in the Wigner space
  have h₂ : (fun ω : Index n → ℝ ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω : Index n → ℝ ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
    <;>
    aesop
  -- Use the fact that the two functions are the same to conclude the proof
  rw [h₂]
  exact h₁

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
    <;>
    aesop
  
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [h₁]
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₄ : (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) = WignerMeasure μ ν n := rfl
      -- Use the fact that the projection has the law of the corresponding measure in the product
      have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
        apply Measure.hasLaw_pi
      -- Simplify the measure for the diagonal element
      have h₆ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n) = ν := by
        simp [wignerMeasureDist]
        <;>
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;>
        aesop
      -- Rewrite the law using the simplified measure
      rw [h₆] at h₅
      -- Use the fact that the product measure is the Wigner measure
      simpa [h₄] using h₅
    exact h₃
  
  apply h₂

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    by_cases h : i = i <;>
    simp_all [h]
    <;>
    aesop
  
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₃ : (fun ω : Index n → ℝ ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) = (fun ω : Index n → ℝ ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := rfl
    rw [h₃]
    -- Use the fact that the measure on the product space assigns the measure ν to the diagonal entries
    have h₄ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) := rfl
    rw [h₄]
    -- The pi measure assigns the measure ν to each coordinate
    have h₅ : (fun ω : Index n → ℝ ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) = (fun ω : Index n → ℝ ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := rfl
    rw [h₅]
    -- Use the property of the product measure to get the law of the coordinate
    have h₆ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;>
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;>
      aesop
    -- The law of the coordinate is ν
    have h₇ : HasLaw (fun ω : Index n → ℝ ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
      apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi_fst
    rw [h₆] at h₇
    exact h₇
  exact h₂

have h₁ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := by sorry
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by sorry
  have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by sorry
  exact h₃

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
    <;> aesop
  
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (ω : WignerSpace n) (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (ω : WignerSpace n) (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      apply Measure.pi_hasLaw
      <;> simp [wingerMeasureDist]
      <;> try infer_instance
      <;> try norm_num
      <;> try simp_all
      <;> try aesop
    simpa using h₃
  
  have h₃ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n) = ν := by
    simp [wingerMeasureDist, Index]
    <;>
    aesop
  
  have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [h₁] at *
    rw [h₃] at h₂
    exact h₂
  
  exact h₄

have h₂ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := by
      funext ω
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₂]
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the fact that the projection of the Wigner measure onto the diagonal entry is ν
      have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        -- Use the product measure property to extract the law of a single coordinate
        have h₅ : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
          -- The Wigner measure is a probability measure
          have h₅₁ : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
            rw [WignerMeasure]
            apply MeasureTheory.Measure.pi.instIsProbabilityMeasure
            intro p
            by_cases h₅₂ : p.val.1 = p.val.2
            · -- Case: p.val.1 = p.val.2
              simp_all [wignerMeasureDist]
              <;> infer_instance
            · -- Case: p.val.1 ≠ p.val.2
              simp_all [wignerMeasureDist]
              <;> infer_instance
          exact h₅₁
        -- Use the fact that the projection of the product measure is the corresponding factor measure
        have h₆ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
          -- Use the product measure property to extract the law of a single coordinate
          apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi
          <;> simp [wignerMeasureDist]
          <;> aesop
        -- Show that the measure on the diagonal entry is ν
        have h₇ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ = ν := by
          simp [wignerMeasureDist]
          <;> aesop
        rw [h₇] at h₆
        exact h₆
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h2 : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i i : ℝ)) := by
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap']
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Matrix.of_apply])
      <;>
      (try aesop)
    rw [h2]
    have h3 : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i i : ℝ)) = (fun ω ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) := by
      funext ω
      simp [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Index.mk.injEq])
      <;>
      (try aesop)
    rw [h3]
    have h4 : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h5 : (WignerMeasure μ ν n : Measure (WignerSpace n)) = Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) := by rfl
      rw [h5]
      have h6 : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) = ν := by
        simp [wignerMeasureDist]
        <;>
        (try simp_all [Index.mk.injEq])
        <;>
        (try aesop)
      have h7 : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) ν (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
        have h8 : IsProbabilityMeasure ν := by infer_instance
        have h9 : IsProbabilityMeasure μ := by infer_instance
        apply MeasureTheory.fun_wise_hasLaw_of_L_set
        <;> simp_all [wignerMeasureDist]
        <;>
        (try aesop)
      exact h7
    exact h4
  exact h1

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    (try cases n <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero]) <;>
    (try { split_ifs <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff] <;> norm_num <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try { linarith }) }) <;>
    (try { aesop })
    <;>
    (try { rfl })
    <;>
    (try {
      exfalso
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;> omega
    })
  
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₃ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) := rfl
    rw [h₃]
    -- Use the product measure property to get the law of the diagonal entry
    have h₄ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) := rfl
    rw [h₄]
    -- Use the fact that the measure is a product measure and the diagonal entry is one of the factors
    have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      apply MeasurableSpace.measurableMeasurable_fun_hasLaw
    -- Simplify the measure to ν
    have h₆ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n) = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;>
      (try { aesop }) <;>
      (try {
        cases n <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero]
        <;> aesop
      })
    rw [h₆] at h₅
    exact h₅
  
  exact h₂

have h₁ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := by sorry
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by sorry
  have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by sorry
  exact h₃

have h₁ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), (by simp)⟩ : Index n)) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      have h₂ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) (⟨(i, i), by simp⟩ : Index n) = ν := by
        simp [wignerMeasureDist]
        <;> aesop
      -- The coordinate projection of a product measure inherits the measure of the component
      have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), by simp⟩ : Index n)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n (⟨(i, i), by simp⟩ : Index n)) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_mk_pi
        <;> simp_all [wignerMeasureDist]
      rw [h₂] at h₃
      exact h₃
    -- Show that (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i = ω (⟨(i, i), by simp⟩ : Index n)
    have h₄ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), (by simp)⟩ : Index n)) := by
      ext ω
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try ring_nf) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try ring_nf) <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      aesop
    -- Substitute the equality into the HasLaw statement
    rw [h₄]
    exact h₁
  exact h_main

-- The entry (i,i) is exactly the value of the random variable at the diagonal index (i,i)
    have h₂ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) := by
      funext ω
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₂]
    -- The value at (i,i) is a diagonal entry, hence it has distribution ν
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      apply hasLaw_pi _ (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) (WignerMeasure μ ν n)
      <;> simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
    <;> aesop
  
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- Use the property of product measures to extract the law of the diagonal entry
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_fst
      <;>
      (try simp_all [WignerMeasure, Measure.pi])
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try simp_all [wignerMeasureDist, Index])
    -- Since ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ is a diagonal pair, the measure is ν
    have h₄ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist, Index]
      <;>
      aesop
    rw [h₄] at h₃
    exact h₃
  
  exact h₂

(fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := by
    ext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
    <;> aesop
  rw [h₁]
  -- We need to show that the function ω ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ has the law ν.
  -- Since ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ is in the index set, and the diagonal entries have ν as their law,
  -- we can directly use the properties of the WignerMeasure to conclude that the law is ν.
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- Use the fact that the coordinate function in the product measure space has the law ν.
    have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Apply the theorem about coordinate functions in product measure spaces.
      apply Measureموقع.hasLaw_pi
      <;> simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> aesop
    -- Since ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ is a diagonal entry, its measure is ν.
    have h₄ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₄] at h₃
    exact h₃
  exact h₂

have h1 : (fun ω : WignerSpace n => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) =
      (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> aesop
  rw [h1]
  -- Use the fact that the measure is the product measure over Index n
  have h2 : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- Apply the product measure property
    have h3 : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_coe _
      <;> exact inferInstance
    -- Show that the measure on the diagonal is ν
    have h4 : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h4] at h3
    exact h3
  exact h2

-- Define the projection function onto the diagonal entry
    let π : WignerSpace n → ℝ := fun ω ↦ ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)
    -- Show that the diagonal entry is equivalent to the projection
    have h₁ : (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = π := by
      funext ω
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Index]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₁]
    -- Use the fact that the projection is independent and has the correct law
    have h₂ : HasLaw π ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the measure-theoretic properties of the WignerMeasure
      have h₃ : HasLaw π ν (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
        -- The projection is one of the coordinates in the product space
        have h₄ : HasLaw (fun x : Index n → ℝ => x (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ : Index n)) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n)) := by
          apply Measure.pi_hasLaw_apply
        -- The measure for the diagonal entry is ν
        have h₅ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ = ν := by
          dsimp [wignerMeasureDist]
          simp [Fin.ext_iff]
          <;> aesop
        rw [h₅] at h₄
        exact h₄
      -- The WignerMeasure is the product measure
      have h₆ : WignerMeasure μ ν n = Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n) := rfl
      rw [h₆] at *
      exact h₃
    exact h₂
  exact h_main

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction, le_refl]
    <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
    <;> split_ifs <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})
    <;> try contradiction
    <;> try linarith
    <;> try omega
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₃ : (WignerMeasure μ ν n) = Measure.pi (fun (p : Index n) => if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ν else μ) := rfl
    rw [h₃]
    have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (if (⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩ : Index n).val.2 then ν else μ) (Measure.pi (fun (p : Index n) => if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ν else μ)) := by
      have h₅ : HasLaw (fun ω : (Index n → ℝ) ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (if (⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩ : Index n).val.2 then ν else μ) (Measure.pi (fun (p : Index n) => if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ν else μ)) := by
        apply MeasureTheory.Measure.pi.hasLaw_π
        <;> simp
        <;> aesop
      exact h₅
    have h₆ : (if (⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩ : Index n).val.1 = (⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩ : Index n).val.2 then ν else μ) = ν := by
      simp [Index]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h₆] at h₄
    exact h₄
  exact h₂

rw [WignerMeasure]
    have h₂ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;> aesop
    simp_all [h₂]
    <;> apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_single
    <;> simp_all [MeasureTheory.isProbabilityMeasure_iff, MeasureTheory.hasLaw_single]
    <;> aesop
  have h₂ : (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = (fun ω : Index n → ℝ => ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) := by
    funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;>
    (try { cases n <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ] <;> aesop }) <;>
    (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, le_refl] <;> aesop }) <;>
    (try { aesop }) <;>
    (try { simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, le_of_lt] <;> aesop })
    <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;>
      (try { omega }) <;>
      (try { aesop })
    })
  rw [h₂]
  exact h₁

have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- The diagonal entries follow ν, so we can directly conclude
      sorry
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      -- Use the fact that the coordinate projection has law ν
      have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
        -- Apply the product measure property for coordinate projections
        have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
          apply (measureTheory_hasProductMeasuremap _ _).hasLawProduct_dite' ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩
        exact h₄
      have h₅ : wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ = ν := by
        -- Since the index is diagonal, its measure is ν
        simp [wignerMeasureDist, le_refl]
        <;>
          (try decide) <;>
          (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
          (try omega)
      rw [h₅] at h₃
      exact h₃
    have h₆ : (fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i i) = fun ω : WignerSpace n ↦ ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ := by
      funext ω
      simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      <;> split_ifs <;>
        (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try {
          exfalso
          simple_omega
        })
    rw [h₆] at *
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

funext ω
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
    <;> aesop
  
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) ν (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    have h₃ : (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) = ν := by
      simp [wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> aesop
    
    have h₄ : HasLaw (fun ω : WignerSpace n => ω ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (wignerMeasureDist μ ν n ⟨(i, i), by simp [le_refl]⟩) (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
      apply MeasureTheory.hasLaw_pi
      <;> simp [h₃]
    
    rw [h₃] at h₄
    exact h₄
  
  exact h₂
